themeName: Sundown Spirits: Craft Cocktail Discovery at Sea

visual.aestheticLabel: Sundown Spirits: Modern Aperitif at Sea

typographyDirection.headlineStyle: Brand-first geometric sans, tight caps with airy tracking; elegant, modern aperitif energy.
typographyDirection.bodyStyle: Humanist sans with calm rhythm for relaxed, sea-day reading.

visual.imageryMood: Ocean-forward serenity with glassware sparkleâgolden hour easing into blue-hour hush; salt-air poise over lively decks.
visual.lightingStyle: Warm low-angle sun with cool navy shadows; soft specular highlights on cut glass; after-dusk pools of tungsten warmth against deep cobalt sky.
visual.compositionNotes: Cruise-first frames: horizon and raillines, open deck banquettes, lounge windows. Keep one subtle cue (a single spritz, a neat garnish, a tasteful menu card). Two to three guests max, relaxed posture, soft eye contact, generous negative space. Avoid crowded tabletops or staged setups; let the shipâs architecture and sea carry the scene.
avoidList: Classroom rows, whiteboards, or pointer props
Lab coats, spittoons, or exam sheets
Lanyards or table tents in focus
referenceMoodboard: Oasis-class open deck at golden hour, rail catching last light
Blue-hour reflection in a windowed lounge, distant harbor glow
Cut-crystal highball with a single expressed orange peel
The Lime & Coconut terrace with sea breeze and soft palm shadows
Menu corner: tidy serif numbers, flavor-journey groupings, ample white space

plausibilityFramework.governingPrinciple: If the frame reads as an outstanding cruise moment without the cocktail story, keep itâthen layer the aperitif identity through one quiet, guest-led cue.
cruiseNativeMoments: Golden-hour rail time on Symphony of the Seas (Oasis-class)
Pre-dinner lounge seats with a sea-facing window
The Lime & Coconut terrace at sunset
Casual poolside shade during sea days
After-dinner drift in a quiet bar nook
nicheEnhancedMoments: A single spritz lifted at the rail as wake foam trails below, citrus twist catching sun
Three small pours on a standard bar mat with a tiny origin card; guests linger and chat with the bartender between normal service
A discreet garnish cart gliding past Promenade seating; a vermouth bottle on ice and a handful of fresh peels
Menu page grouped from âBitter to Brightâ on a two-top by the window, fingers tracing choices
Nightcap negroni riff resting on a coaster while two guests talk, shoulders angled toward the horizon
implausibleLiteralizations: Technique-heavy lessons with whiteboards or tasting grids
Guest flair competitions requiring practice or staging
Hours-long spirits history seminars with slides
allowedProps: Elegant stemware and balloon spritz glasses
Single citrus peel or olive pick
Low-profile garnish caddy or small ice bucket
Paper cocktail napkin with a tiny campaign mark
discouragedProps: Tripods, ring lights, or mic stands
Classroom mats or numbered flights with grid scoring
Large banners, table tents, or wayfinding signage
Barrel props, micro-stills, or chemistry setups

messaging.subSlogan: Modern aperitif mood aboard Symphony of the Seas â easy discovery woven into sailaway, sunset, and nightcaps.
messaging.elevatorPitch: Sundown Spirits turns the cruise moments you already loveâsailaway, sunset drift, pre-dinner pause, late-night winkâinto gentle craft-cocktail discovery. On Symphony of the Seas, bartenders fold in a small three-pour story when the moment fits, spritz-forward welcomes, and playful negroni riffsâwithout turning your vacation into a class. Dip in as you please and let the Western Caribbean horizon do the rest.
messaging.voicePersona: That clever bar-friend who knows the menu by heart and still keeps it easyâcurious, warm, never instructional.

merch.conceptStatement: Playful, print-friendly aperitivo-at-sea tees that wink at low-ABV sparkle, negroni culture, and horizon-time without feeling boozy or fussy.

audio.ambientNarrationScript: The sky leans gold, the glass catches light, and the ship eases toward blue hour. On Symphony of the Seas, aperitif discovery arrives softlyâa spritz at sailaway, a few quiet stories at sunset, a late-night riff that lingers. No keeping time. Just horizon, good company, and pours that drift from bitter to bright.
audio.hypeClipScript: Glasses clink. Wake rolls. Sundown Spirits on Symphony of the Seasâsix nights, Western Caribbean. Spritz at sailaway, three pours at sunset, negroni after dark. Bitter to brightâcarried by the horizon.
audio.voiceProfile: Warm, velvety baritone or contralto; intimate, unhurried; understated smile at the ends of lines.
audio.musicMood: Modern lounge and nuâjazz at sunset (90â105 BPM), brushed drums and Rhodes; late-night negroni mood shifts to 70â85 BPM with soft analog keys and vibraphone; light Caribbean breeze textures without resorting to clichÃ©s.

narrative.title: Rail Lean, Citrus Air
narrative.scriptOrNarration: Symphony of the Seas does this quiet magic at sunsetâocean first, conversation easy, pours that drift from bitter to bright. No keeping time. Just gold to blue and good company.

narrative.musicMood: Nuâjazz Rhodes at ~96 BPM; brushed drums, light seaâbreeze foley.
